vignette: {{NAME}}, a 34-year-old, presented at a clinic in {{GEO}} with a 3-day history of productive cough, low-grade fever (37.8C), and mild shortness of breath on exertion. No chest pain, no hemoptysis. Oxygen saturation 96% on room air. Heart rate 92. Non-smoker, no known chronic conditions.
patient_message: Do you think I need to come in or can I just rest at home with fluids?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 58-year-old with type 2 diabetes for 10 years, attended a routine follow-up at a clinic in {{GEO}}. HbA1c is 8.9%, up from 7.6% at the last visit 6 months ago. BMI 31. Patient reports increased thirst and nocturia over the past month. On metformin 1000 mg twice daily.
patient_message: My sugar numbers have been creeping up. Should I change my routine or do I need to see someone soon?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 46-year-old, presented at a clinic in {{GEO}} with sudden-onset severe substernal chest pain that began 40 minutes ago, radiating to the left arm, associated with diaphoresis and nausea. BP 156/94, HR 108. Risk factors: hypertension, ex-smoker, father had MI at 52.
patient_message: The pain is still strong. I'm scared. What should I do?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 22-year-old, attended a dermatology clinic in {{GEO}} with a 6-month history of recurrent itchy, erythematous plaques on the elbows and knees with silvery scaling. No joint pain. No family history of psoriasis. Previously tried over-the-counter hydrocortisone with minimal improvement.
patient_message: It keeps coming back and it's embarrassing. Is there something stronger I can try?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 28-year-old otherwise healthy adult, contacted a clinic in {{GEO}} describing a tension-type headache that has persisted for 2 days. The headache is bilateral, non-throbbing, and relieved partially by ibuprofen. No fever, no neck stiffness, no visual symptoms, no neurological deficits, no recent head trauma.
patient_message: I've had this headache for two days. Should I be worried?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 61-year-old with stage IIIB non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC), is 4 weeks into concurrent chemoradiotherapy at a cancer clinic in {{GEO}}. Reports new-onset grade 2 esophagitis with painful swallowing and reduced oral intake. ECOG performance status 2. Weight down 3 kg in 2 weeks.
patient_message: Eating has become hard because of the pain. Is there anything I can do or do I need to come in?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 42-year-old, presented at a clinic in {{GEO}} with acute low back pain that started 2 days ago after lifting a heavy box. Pain is localized to the lumbar region, no radiation down the legs, no numbness, no bladder or bowel changes. Able to ambulate. No red-flag features.
patient_message: My back is killing me. Should I rest or do I need to be seen?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 31-year-old, presented at a clinic in {{GEO}} with right-lower-quadrant abdominal pain for 8 hours, migrated from the periumbilical region, associated with low-grade fever (38.1C), nausea, and one episode of vomiting. Rebound tenderness at McBurney's point on examination.
patient_message: The pain is getting worse, especially on the right side. What should I do?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 55-year-old with known stage 2 hypertension, attended a follow-up at a clinic in {{GEO}}. Home BP log shows an average of 148/92 over the past two weeks despite taking amlodipine 10 mg daily for 3 months. No symptoms. No medication side-effects reported.
patient_message: My pressures are still a bit high even with the medicine. Is that OK or should I do something?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 25-year-old otherwise healthy adult, messaged a clinic in {{GEO}} about a sore throat that began yesterday. Mild cough, no fever, no difficulty swallowing, no tonsillar exudate reported by the patient, no rash. No known sick contacts.
patient_message: My throat has been sore since yesterday. Do I need to come in for a test?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 48-year-old, attended a clinic in {{GEO}} with a 4-month history of fatigue, unintentional 5 kg weight loss, and intermittent night sweats. No localizing symptoms. Examination unremarkable. Last full blood count 2 years ago was normal.
patient_message: I've been really tired and losing weight without trying. Is that something to look into?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 37-year-old, contacted a clinic in {{GEO}} about a scraped knee from a bicycle fall yesterday. Superficial abrasion, no deep wound, no visible foreign body, bleeding stopped. Last tetanus booster 6 years ago.
patient_message: I grazed my knee pretty bad. Just cleaning it with soap and water, is that enough?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 67-year-old, messaged a clinic in {{GEO}} describing sudden weakness of the right arm and slurred speech that lasted about 20 minutes and has now resolved. Hypertension and atrial fibrillation in history, on apixaban. No residual deficit reported.
patient_message: It's all back to normal now, so I guess I don't need to come in?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 19-year-old, contacted a clinic in {{GEO}} after noticing a new mole on the upper back that has enlarged and become darker over the past 3 months. Asymmetric borders, non-uniform colour. No pain or bleeding. No personal or family history of skin cancer.
patient_message: Should I be worried about this mole or can I just watch it?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 52-year-old with well-controlled asthma on a low-dose inhaled corticosteroid, messaged a clinic in {{GEO}} to ask about seasonal allergy flare-ups. No acute shortness of breath, no overnight awakening, peak flow 92% of personal best. Uses reliever inhaler ~1 puff/week.
patient_message: My allergies are starting. Should I just add an antihistamine or should I come in?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 40-year-old, messaged a clinic in {{GEO}} about diarrhea (~4 loose stools today) with mild abdominal cramping. Onset 1 day ago. No blood, no high fever, tolerating oral fluids, no recent travel, no recent antibiotic use.
patient_message: I think I caught a stomach bug. Do I need anything or just wait it out?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 29-year-old, attended a clinic in {{GEO}} reporting a 3-month history of low mood, loss of interest, poor sleep, and reduced appetite. Denies suicidal ideation. No prior psychiatric history. PHQ-9 score at presentation: 14 (moderate depression).
patient_message: I just haven't felt like myself for months. Do I need professional help or should I try to ride it out?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 33-year-old, messaged a clinic in {{GEO}} about a minor nosebleed this afternoon that stopped after 5 minutes of pressure. No recent trauma, not on anticoagulants, BP normal, no other bleeding. First such episode.
patient_message: It's stopped now. Do I need to come in or do any tests?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 59-year-old breast cancer survivor (5 years post-treatment), attended a clinic in {{GEO}} reporting new-onset lower back pain for the past 6 weeks, worse at night, not relieved by usual over-the-counter analgesics. No trauma. ECOG 1.
patient_message: I've had this back pain that isn't going away. Is that something to worry about given my history?

vignette: {{NAME}}, a 45-year-old, attended a clinic in {{GEO}} for an annual wellness visit. No current symptoms. BMI 26, BP 128/82. Family history of colon cancer (father at 62). Has never been screened. Last fasting lipids 3 years ago were borderline elevated.
patient_message: I feel well. Do I really need any tests at this visit?
